title: EnergAI: A Large Language Model-Driven Generative Design Method for Early-Stage Building Energy Optimization
contribution: 开发了EnergAI框架，使用LLM和分层提示策略生成低能耗建筑设计方案，性能导向提示下平均EUI降低50%以上，GPT-4o达63.3%
abstract: This paper proposes EnergAI, a generative design framework that incorporates energy optimization objectives directly into the scheme generation process through large language models (e.g., GPT-4o, DeepSeek-V3.1-Think, Qwen-Max, and Gemini-2.5 pro). A dedicated dataset, LowEnergy-FormNet, comprising 2160 cases with site parameters, massing descriptors, and simulation outputs, was constructed to model site, form, and energy relationships. Geometry-oriented and fuzzy-goal prompts achieve average annual reductions of approximately 16–17% in energy use intensity and 3–4% in energy cost compared with human designs, while performance-oriented structured prompts eliminate high-energy outliers and yield an average EUI-saving rate above 50%. In cross-model comparisons, GPT-4o achieved 63.3% mean EUI savings, nearly 13% higher than DeepSeek-V3.1-Think, Qwen-Max, and Gemini-2.5 baselines.
methodology: Builds the LowEnergy-FormNet dataset of 2160 cases and encodes building massing into parametric vectors, then employs a hierarchical prompt strategy framework (geometry-oriented, fuzzy-goal, and performance-oriented prompts) to drive LLM-based form generation within a closed-loop workflow coupled with ClimateStudio energy simulation.
results: Geometry-oriented and fuzzy-goal prompts achieve average annual reductions of approximately 16–17% in EUI and 3–4% in energy cost compared to human designs. Performance-oriented structured prompts eliminate high-energy outliers and yield an average EUI reduction above 50%. GPT-4o achieves 63.3% mean EUI savings, outperforming DeepSeek-V3.1-Think (~50.5%), Qwen-Max (~50.7%), and Gemini-2.5 pro (~49.9%) under identical conditions.
